summary:
The carotid sinus containing baroreceptors is primarily located at the bifurcation of the common carotid artery, specifically where it divides into the internal and external branches. This structural feature places it within the common carotid artery’s terminal section—not solely within the internal carotid—as confirmed by authoritative medical literature emphasizing the origin at the bifurcation point.
